note: The creative tool knowledge base for Orive Creative Studio. Structured data the host and every specialist read to recommend, check, and install the right tool. This is tier-zero seeded knowledge, no web search needed to use it.
refresh: Re-check the source repos and connector pages quarterly, not on every call. Only search when verifying that a repo, installer, or MCP URL has changed.

tools.name: Adobe Creative Cloud

install.steps: Open Customize, go to Connectors, click +, find Adobe, sign in with your Adobe ID.

tools.free_tier: about 40 tools as guest, full 50 plus with sign-in

tools.name: Higgsfield

install.steps_chat: Open Customize, go to Connectors, click +, select Add custom connector, name it Higgsfield, paste https://mcp.higgsfield.ai/mcp as the URL, click Connect, sign in with your Higgsfield account.
install.command_claude_code: claude mcp add higgsfield --transport http https://mcp.higgsfield.ai/mcp

tools.free_tier: new accounts ship with free credits
tools.costs: credit-based per generation, varies by model and resolution
tools.capabilities: AI video up to 4K and up to 15 seconds, AI image up to 4K, character consistency via Soul training
tools.read_and_stage_note: Each generation costs credits. Show the model and the estimated cost before generating. Never auto-generate without the user saying yes.

tools.name: DaVinci Resolve

install.steps: Clone samuelgursky/davinci-resolve-mcp, run python install.py. It auto-configures the client.
install.command_claude_code: git clone https://github.com/samuelgursky/davinci-resolve-mcp && cd davinci-resolve-mcp && python install.py

tools.requires: Resolve Studio (not the free edition), Python 3.10 plus, external scripting set to Local in Resolve preferences

tools.name: Blender
tools.type: community MCP plus official Blender lab server

install.steps: Download addon.py from ahujasid/blender-mcp, install it in Blender addons, add the uvx server to .mcp.json.

tools.name: Affinity
tools.type: community MCP, drives the UI via AppleScript on macOS or registry automation on Windows

install.steps: Clone sekharmalla/affinity-mcp-server, run bun install, add it to .mcp.json, grant Accessibility permission in System Settings.

tools.requires: Affinity installed, macOS for best support
tools.maturity: early, UI-driven not API-driven, slower and more fragile

install.steps: Already connected. If not, open Customize, go to Connectors, click +, find Figma, sign in.
Already connected. If not, open Customize, go to Connectors, click +, find Canva, sign in.
Sign up at https://www.pexels.com/api/, get the key, add it to .env as PEXELS_API_KEY.

tools.name: CapCut
tools.location: local, needs CapCut or Jianying (CapCut International) installed

install.steps: Run the studio's CapCut installer, which clones sun-guannan/VectCutAPI into vendor/ (gitignored), builds a Python venv, installs the parts, and uses the capcut server already wired in .mcp.json. Needs CapCut and FFmpeg installed.
install.via_setup: setup.ps1 -WithCapCut  (Windows) or  bash setup.sh --with-capcut  (macOS or Linux)

tools.requires: CapCut installed, Python 3.10 plus, FFmpeg on PATH
tools.capabilities: Builds CapCut draft projects programmatically: video, audio, and image tracks, text and captions, transitions, effects, and animations, then opens the draft in CapCut to finish and export.
tools.maturity: community, no official CapCut MCP exists. VectCutAPI (formerly CapCutAPI) is the established engine and ships its own stdio MCP server. For a richer Claude Code layer with presets and talking-head workflows, MigueDuque/capcut-mcp-server-extended adds a TypeScript MCP on top of the same VectCutAPI backend.

rules.generation_first: When the task is make a video or generate an image and no existing footage or photo is involved, recommend Higgsfield first. It is the fastest path from idea to visual because it runs cloud-side with no local app.
rules.edit_existing: If the user already has footage or photos to edit, recommend DaVinci or Adobe instead. If they need 3D, recommend Blender. Higgsfield generates, the others edit.
rules.chain: When a task needs both generation and editing, recommend the chain and name the handoff. Example: Higgsfield to generate a product video, then DaVinci or Adobe to grade it.
rules.least_needed: Never recommend more than the task needs. If Canva handles it, do not suggest Adobe. If Figma is connected and the task is UI, do not suggest Affinity.
